sentence: INTRODUCTION It is quite clear that Asia is still some distance away from full recovery.
label: neutral

sentence: However, financial stability is slowly being regained.
label: positive

sentence: The painful process of adjustment is most evident in the sharp improvement in external current account balances that have been principally realized through significant cutbacks in imports and economic recessions.
label: negative

sentence: At the same time, Asian governments have actively sought to rapidly rebuild confidence by putting in place major reforms especially in their financial systems which bore the brunt of the bursting of the Asian asset bubble.
label: neutral

sentence: Smart investors are seeing in all of these an opportunity to position early and take advantage of an expected Asian economic comeback.
label: neutral

sentence: The Philippines has been more fortunate in avoiding the worst effects of the Asian crisis although it has not been unscathed.
label: positive

sentence: Moreover, we have not lost time in doing what we have to do to build better economic foundations.
label: positive

sentence: THE ROLE OF THE BSP In line with our mandate, the BSP has been at the forefront of the Philippine team effort to secure overall financial stability during this crisis period.
label: positive

sentence: We have also initiated major reforms to protect the health of the banking system amid mounting pressures.
label: positive

sentence: One of the strongest institutional features in the Philippines is the independence of the BSP that is directly guaranteed by the Philippine Constitution.
label: positive

sentence: This has given us the ability to respond more objectively, more quickly and more decisively to changing economic conditions as circumstances require.
label: positive

sentence: This independence has been especially useful in taking decisive actions even during a period of political transition in the Philippines.
label: positive

sentence: THE KEY RESULT AREAS Going by the results, the Philippines has not done too badly given the depth of the Asian crisis.
label: positive

sentence: In contrast with most of the other affected economies in the region, the Philippines may yet avoid a full-blown economic recession.
label: positive

sentence: We will also likely meet our revised inflation target of 9.5 percent despite the adverse impact of the El Niño phenomenon and recent destructive typhoons on food prices.
label: positive

sentence: Interest rates have also moderated significantly.
label: positive

sentence: Benchmark bank lending rates are currently at around 17-18 percent versus around 30 percent earlier this year.
label: neutral

sentence: Before the crisis, these were at 13-14 percent.
label: neutral

sentence: The peso has also recently rallied to around P40-P41 to the U.S. dollar, broadly in line with the cumulative depreciations of the Korean won, the Thai baht, and the Malaysian ringgit.
label: neutral

sentence: In the external account, the underlying adjustment process is going very well.
label: positive

sentence: As of January to August 1998, the current account balance registered a small surplus of $38 million versus a deficit of $3.4 billion over the same period last year.
label: positive

sentence: More encouraging, merchandise export growth has remained strong at close to 19 percent and this has been backed up by sustained inflows of workers remittances.
label: positive

sentence: The decline of imports has also been much more moderate than in other countries which partly explains why economic output in the Philippines has not collapsed as much.
label: positive

sentence: In recognition of the real progress that has been achieved, the IMF Executive Board last October 30 completed its review of the country's economic performance and policies.
label: positive

sentence: It also approved the release of a $280 million initial drawing against a total stand-by facility of about $1.4 billion.
label: positive

sentence: Significantly, in endorsing the country's economic policies, the IMF recognized the necessity of allowing greater fiscal flexibility to avoid a recession.
label: neutral

sentence: STATUS OF THE BANKING SYSTEM At this point, I would now like to devote some time to the banking situation in the Philippines.
label: neutral

sentence: As we all know, this area has been the object of much concern in countries around the region as a consequence of the Asian crisis.
label: neutral

sentence: I think it is useful to start by understanding the structure of our banking system to appreciate the relative importance of specific banking institutions from the point of view of overall systemic risk.
label: neutral

sentence: This is critical to putting incidences of bank failure in proper perspective.
label: neutral

sentence: In our system, the relatively much smaller rural banks and thrift banks are numerically dominant but they account for a mere 9 percent of total system resources.
label: neutral

sentence: However, the commercial banking system, consisting of 53 operating banks at present, account for 91 percent of total resources.
label: neutral

sentence: The commercial banking system also accounts for nearly two-thirds of total banking offices nationwide.
label: neutral

sentence: Relatively speaking, current banking problems here are relatively limited and much smaller compared to other affected countries.
label: positive

sentence: They do not constitute a systemic crisis such as are being experienced now in Japan, Indonesia, Thailand, Korea and, perhaps, Malaysia and China.
label: positive

sentence: To begin with, the bad loan problem here is still relatively much milder despite a significant deterioration as a result of the Asian crisis.
label: neutral

sentence: The non-performing loan ratio for all commercial banks was measured at 10.5 percent of total loans as of August 1998.
label: neutral

sentence: However, this compares with 75 percent in Indonesia, 50 percent in Thailand, 45 percent in Korea, and 35 percent in Malaysia according to one private estimate (Warburg Dillon Read).
label: positive

sentence: Moreover, our commercial banks have already faced up to the higher credit risk by setting up loss provisions equivalent to about 29 percent of estimated non-performing loans.
label: positive

sentence: There are several reasons for our banks' better asset quality and stronger balance sheet.
label: positive

sentence: First, the asset bubble here was smaller since we received a smaller share of the massive capital inflows to the region especially in the 1990s.
label: positive

sentence: Second, since our banking industry is private sector-dominated, banks have been able to exercise more objective and critical credit judgment that also reflected their experience, especially bad experience in the 1980s.
label: positive

sentence: Third, the BSP has been mindful of its job of preserving the stability of the banking system through effective regulation and supervision.
label: positive

sentence: Even before the onset of the Asian crisis, the BSP already adopted some defensive measures to protect the banking system.
label: positive

sentence: This included a 20 percent limit on real estate lending relative to loan portfolio, reduction of allowable real estate collateral valuation from 70 percent to 60 percent, and a 30 percent liquidity requirement on foreign assets.
label: negative

sentence: In 1996, the BSP also ordered a phased increase to be completed by December 1998 in the minimum capitalization requirement of universal banks by 80 percent, of regular commercial banks by 60 percent and of the bigger Metro-Manila based thrift banks by 67 percent.
label: positive

sentence: As a result, our banking system today has one of the highest capital adequacy ratios in the region, at over 17 percent of risk assets as of June 1998.
label: positive

sentence: This is comparable to those of Singapore and Hong Kong.
label: neutral

sentence: Moreover, our capital ratios are well in excess of either our 10 percent minimum requirement as prescribed in the General Banking Act or the minimum of 8 percent as recommended by the BIS.
label: positive

sentence: This situation enables our banks to better cope, on their own, with possibly higher loan losses due to a slowing economy.
label: positive

sentence: Since the start of the Asian crisis (July 1997), one commercial bank and 7 thrift banks have failed.
label: negative

sentence: However, their combined deposits of P4.7 billion account for slightly higher than one-fourth of one percent (0.27 percent) of the total deposits of the banking system of P1.7 trillion.
label: positive

sentence: Their combined assets are equivalent to only about 0.41 percent of GDP.
label: neutral

sentence: These are far too insignificant to threaten the banking system.
label: positive

sentence: Because we have successfully avoided a banking crisis, we do not need to spend massive amounts of public funds to clean up bad loans and recapitalize our banking system.
label: positive

sentence: Government financial support to the banking system has been primarily in the form of fully collateralized emergency loans provided to stabilize banks when bank runs have occurred due to panic.
label: neutral

sentence: A basically healthy banking system has also meant that our banks are able to operate normally.
label: positive

sentence: Both peso and dollar deposits continue to grow.
label: positive

sentence: On the other hand, net domestic credits to the private sector has slowed down to 9.1 percent as of August 1998 but it remains positive.
label: neutral

sentence: This situation can hardly qualify as a credit crunch where credit supply essentially dries up.
label: neutral

sentence: Moreover, overall credit growth picked up in August versus July.
label: positive

sentence: Further credit expansion can be expected as the agriculture sector and real estate sector recover.
label: positive

sentence: MEASURES TO STRENGTHEN FURTHER THE BANKING SYSTEM The BSP has adopted new banking reforms since the crisis.
label: positive

sentence: The major new measures included: (1) a further capital increase ranging from 20 to 60 percent over 1998 minimum levels to be completed by year 2000; (2) a 2 percent general loan loss provisioning requirement; (3) stricter specific loan loss provisioning requirement; (4) consolidated supervision of banking units and their affiliates; (5) enhanced disclosure requirements; and (6) more stringent bank licensing requirements.
label: positive

sentence: These reforms are strongly endorsed by both the IMF and the World Bank.
label: positive

sentence: We have recently agreed with the World Bank on a banking sector reform loan to further support banking reforms.
label: positive

sentence: Commenting on the Philippine banking situation, Mr. Wolfensohn, President of the World Bank, when he visited here early this year remarked, "... What is very clear is the Philippine situation is vastly different from Korea, Thailand and Indonesia ... I think you all know you are weakened far less than anyone else in the region with the exception of Singapore ..."
label: positive

sentence: Our Fifth Year In Review Our fifth year was characterized by extraordinary challenges due to the onset of the Asian currency crisis shortly after our anniversary last year.
label: negative

sentence: POSITIVE PROSPECTS AND CHALLENGES FOR THE REGION As the title of this conference indicates, Asia is now on the mend: the region has emerged from two-and-a-half years of arduous maneuvering through rough waters.
label: positive

sentence: The foundations for broad-based economic recovery in the region are beginning to be laid.
label: positive

sentence: Recent trends continue to support this view.
label: neutral

sentence: Starting the second quarter of this year, there has been an upturn in domestic demand along with increased industrial activity, rising exports and an improving investment climate.
label: positive

sentence: Relative stability in international financial markets has also been restored.
label: positive

sentence: These factors bode well for the region's growth prospects.
label: positive

sentence: However, while affirming these positive prospects, let me stress that a lot of hard work lies ahead to ensure lasting economic growth.
label: neutral

sentence: This conference, which seeks to provide Asia's central bankers the opportunity to discuss options for monetary policy regimes in an environment, which for most countries involves greater exchange rate flexibility is, therefore, timely.
label: neutral

sentence: Choosing the appropriate monetary policy framework is crucial to preserving monetary stability, establishing stronger macroeconomic fundamentals and sustaining economic growth.
label: neutral

sentence: THE ROLE OF THE MONETARY POLICY IN ECONOMIC GROWTH As you well know, there has been a shift in the understanding of the role of monetary policy in the past two decades.
label: neutral

sentence: Prior to the 1980s, monetary policy was seen as a vehicle for promoting economic development by supporting production and employment.
label: neutral

sentence: Thus, slightly higher inflation, on account of accommodative monetary policy, was considered permissible as it allowed for faster economic growth.
label: neutral

sentence: This old view of the role of monetary policy assumed the existence of a trade-off between inflation, on the one hand, and employment and growth, on the other.
label: neutral

sentence: However, developments in the past twenty years or so have required a review of this paradigm.
label: neutral

sentence: Accommodative monetary policy has almost always led to higher inflation without necessarily promoting economic growth.
label: negative

sentence: The link between expansionary monetary policy--hence higher inflation--and economic growth, if it existed at all, proved to be operative only in the short run.
label: negative

sentence: Thus, we saw the shift in the focus of monetary policy from the promotion of production and employment to maintaining price stability.
label: neutral

sentence: It is widely accepted in policy circles that price stability can be best achieved by constraining discretionary policy, which may give rise to an inflationary bias in the conduct of monetary policy.
label: neutral

sentence: Discretionary policy, in turn, may be curbed through the adoption of a nominal anchor.
label: neutral

sentence: Depending on the choice of a nominal anchor, monetary policy may follow one of four basic approaches: monetary targeting, interest-rate targeting, exchange rate targeting and inflation targeting.
label: neutral

sentence: Nobody but nobody can prescribe what is best for a particular economy except those who have a stake in public policy.
label: neutral

sentence: And the choice will be a function of the behavior of that economy's liquidity as it relates to prices, income and employment; the independence of its monetary authorities; and the extent of development of its financial system as a channel of monetary policy.
label: neutral

sentence: THE CONDUCT OF MONETARY POLICY IN THE PHILIPPINES As the Philippines' central monetary authority, the BSP's primary mandate is to maintain price stability conducive to a balanced and sustainable growth of the economy.
label: neutral

sentence: In fulfilling this mission, the BSP is vested with policy independence by the Philippine constitution itself, as well as the BSP charter.
label: positive

sentence: Given this responsibility, how does the BSP conduct monetary policy?
label: neutral

sentence: The BSP currently follows a monetary targeting framework.
label: neutral

sentence: Under this approach, the demand for money is estimated, given the government's targets for inflation and output growth.
label: neutral

sentence: The appropriate level of money supply is then chosen to match estimated money demand so as to bring about basic equilibrium in the money market.
label: neutral

sentence: To attain the targeted level of money supply, the BSP manages the level of base money (BM), taking into account the link between base money and the money supply.
label: neutral

sentence: This it does by employing a variety of monetary policy instruments.
label: neutral

sentence: Thus far, monetary targeting has worked for us.
label: neutral

sentence: Since the establishment of the Bangko Sentral in 1993, various forms of inflationary pressures have challenged its task of maintaining price stability.
label: negative

sentence: Nevertheless, the BSP has managed to keep inflation within single-digit levels.
label: positive

sentence: I am fully aware, of course, of the other monetary frameworks, including inflation targeting.
label: neutral

sentence: The BSP's efforts to ensure price stability has given it the flexibility to conduct monetary policy in support of the economic recovery.
label: positive

sentence: With inflation within target levels and monetary aggregates below programmed ceilings, adjustments have been made--in the BSP's policy interest rates and the reserve requirements--to lower the cost of lending and to improve the availability of credit.
label: positive

sentence: This monetary stance has helped nurture the economic recovery.
label: positive

sentence: During the first three quarters of the year, the Philippines performed better than expected, with real GNP expanding by 3.0 percent compared to the programmed 1.9 percent.

sentence: Growth was promoted in a low-inflation environment amidst generally stable foreign exchange market conditions, a sound external position and a healthy financial system.
label: positive

sentence: However, successful monetary targeting has certain requisites.
label: negative

sentence: Foremost among these is a stable and predictable relationship between monetary and real sector variables.
label: neutral

sentence: We are well aware, however, that financial deregulation and liberalization have given rise to a new financial architecture and a continuously evolving array of financial products and services.
label: neutral

sentence: These developments have caused large and frequent fluctuations in the velocity of money, thus weakening the link between monetary and real sector variables.
label: negative

sentence: In the Philippines, we have experienced this "de-linking" in at least two instances: first, when inflation remained low, even as monetary aggregates were breached, and second, when inflation continued to accelerate, while monetary aggregates were kept well within targets.
label: negative

sentence: These experiences have led the BSP to explore other options for monetary policy, among them, inflation targeting.

sentence: During the symposium commemorating the 50th anniversary of central banking in the Philippines, which was held in Manila last January, inflation targeting was one of the topics for discussion.
label: neutral

sentence: Governor Donald Brash of the Reserve Bank of New Zealand presented the main paper and I was privileged to have been invited as one of the reactors.

sentence: Let me discuss some of the points I raised in my reaction paper.
label: neutral

sentence: First, inflation here in the Philippines is affected, to a large extent, by non-monetary factors.
label: neutral

sentence: In fact, food accounts for more than 50 percent of our CPI basket.
label: neutral

sentence: Clearly, in our case, inflation cannot be addressed by monetary policy alone.

sentence: On the latter point, it is important to underscore that the effectiveness of monetary policy is strengthened if the goals and instruments of policy are known to the public and if the authorities can make a credible commitment to meeting them.

sentence: REGIONAL COOPERATION As I noted earlier, patterns of recovery are emerging in Asia, with the pace and magnitude of recovery stronger for some countries.
label: positive

sentence: Sustaining the recovery requires firm commitment on the part of the governments in the region to pursue structural reforms, as well as implement consistent macroeconomic policies.
label: neutral

sentence: These twin thrusts will help ensure the soundness of the financial system and create a more robust macroeconomic environment, which will minimize the effects of any external shock on the domestic economy.
label: positive

sentence: However, in an increasingly globalized economic environment, efforts on the national level are unlikely to be very effective in reducing the likelihood of the recurrence of a similar crisis.

sentence: Regional collaboration is crucial.

sentence: Introduction Mr. Adrian H. Thomas, Conference Manager and Head of the Asian Division of Euromoney, distinguished participants to this Seventh Annual Asia Pacific Issuers and Investors Forum organized by Euromoney: Let me begin by expressing my deep appreciation to the conference organizers for inviting me to keynote the Euromoney Investors Forum, which is the leading regional gathering for the capital markets in Asia.

sentence: The Role of the BSP At this point, let me underscore certain distinguishing features of the Philippine monetary system, which served it in good stead in weathering the Asian financial crisis.
label: positive

sentence: As the Philippine central monetary authority, the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) is mandated by the Philippine Constitution to provide policy directions in the areas of money, banking and credit; to supervise the banking system, and to regulate other financial institutions such as finance companies.
label: neutral

sentence: The BSP's primary mission is to deliver price stability conducive to a balanced growth of the economy.
label: neutral

sentence: The BSP enjoys policy independence in the pursuit of this mission.
label: positive

sentence: This is guaranteed not just by ordinary law but by the Philippine constitution itself.
label: neutral

sentence: The onset of the financial crisis in Asia has provided a major challenge to the bank's adherence to this mission, and at times has subjected the bank to criticism and pressure to sacrifice this objective in the pursuit of short-term, more popular goals.
label: negative

sentence: The BSP responded to this challenge by adjusting its monetary and financial policies and striking a delicate balance between restoring order in the foreign exchange market, containing inflationary pressures, and limiting the adverse impact of the crisis on the banking and corporate sectors.

sentence: This policy stance was rewarded by the containment of the annual inflation rate to single digit levels even at the height of the crisis.

sentence: Belying dire predictions arising from the current increases in world oil prices, the general price level has remained broadly stable, with the inflation rate settling at 5.4 percent in October this year, for a year-to-date average of 7.2 percent.

sentence: Economic Growth Performance and Prospects With the economy recovering from the Asian financial storm and with improvement in weather conditions, the Philippines performed better than expected during the first three quarters of this year, with gross national product (GNP) expanding by 3.0 percent in real terms.

sentence: The evidence is now clear that industry has begun to recover.

sentence: Our growth prospects-especially in terms of our exports-have been helped in large part by the recent marked improvement in global economic and financial conditions.
label: positive

sentence: Of late, some concerns have been raised over the eventual demand slowdown of the US economy.
label: negative

sentence: However, with adept management especially by the U.S. Fed, we believe that if there is any slowdown at some point, it will be a soft landing.
label: positive

sentence: This could be offset by the strengthening economies of Japan and Europe, as well as of other Asian countries.
label: positive

sentence: Our commitment to price stability has also paid off, in that the resulting low inflation rates have given monetary policy the necessary flexibility in supporting the economic recovery.
label: positive

sentence: Thus, sustained policy adjustments have been made to lower the cost of lending and to improve the availability of credit.
label: positive

sentence: The benchmark 91-day Treasury bill rate was 8.894 percent at the auction held this Monday [29 November 1999], much lower than the 15.3 percent average for 1998.

sentence: We assure you that at no point will this compromise our inflation targets.

sentence: Facing External Challenges Focusing now on the external sector, risk management will be pursued through the maintenance of an adequate level of foreign exchange reserves and through careful management of our external debt position.
label: positive

sentence: There continues to be broad stability in the foreign exchange market, with reduced market volatility in 1999 relative to 1997 and 1998.
label: positive

sentence: The improvement should continue as regional factors become more positive and as we continue to see a surplus in our external accounts.
label: positive

sentence: For the first ten months of 1999, the balance of payments posted a surplus of US$3.4 billion.
label: positive

sentence: This was a significant increase over the US$1.4 billion surplus recorded during the comparable period in 1998.
label: neutral

sentence: In particular, the current account-buttressed by strong exports-has strengthened considerably, registering a surplus of US$3.3 billion in the first eight months from a surplus of only US$14 million during the same period a year ago.

sentence: As a result, we have been able to build up our foreign exchange reserves to a record high of US$14.6 billion as of the end of October.

sentence: Apart from being a precautionary measure-particularly against the volatile, short-term capital-our reserve build-up program is also a confidence-building measure.

sentence: And let me report to you that our external debt continues to be manageable.
label: positive

label: neutral

sentence: While there has been some increase in the aggregate level, the maturity and creditor profiles have improved.
label: positive

sentence: The share of short-term debt to total debt has declined from 16.4 percent as of end-1992 to 13.6 percent as of the end of the first half of 1999.
label: positive

sentence: Thank you and good day.
label: neutral

sentence: Chief executives, officers and members of the Philippine Association of Hong Kong (PAHK) and the Philippine Bankers Club of Hong Kong (PBHK), good afternoon: Hong Kong is a very suitable place to discuss our country's monetary and fiscal policies.
label: neutral

sentence: This economic entrepot is a bastion of free market principles; and it therefore serves as a very good setting to present our essentially market-based monetary and fiscal policies.
label: positive

sentence: Our meeting this afternoon is also auspicious because we are at such a decisive juncture of the economic recovery of the Philippines when we begin to look ahead to the challenges of the new millennium.
label: neutral

sentence: But let me preface my remarks this afternoon cautioning you that I will be a one-handed speaker; I will focus on monetary policy and touch on, as necessary, -- in passing -- the acts of the fiscal authorities.
label: neutral

sentence: Our responsibilities are sufficiently cut and dried that we can easily discern where one should end and the other should begin.
label: neutral

sentence: Current Economic Policy Current macroeconomic policy aims to foster the recovery and shield against domestic and external shocks.
label: neutral

sentence: We have had our learning curve flattened by our interesting experiences in the 1990s including the surge in foreign money in 1993-97, the Tequila effect of 1994-95 and the Tom Yang effect of 1997-99.
label: neutral

sentence: We have kept the course in the two and a half years of the Asian financial crisis.
label: neutral

sentence: Monetary policy, in particular, has tried to contain inflation while being flexible enough to enable lower interest rates and a stable exchange rate.
label: positive

sentence: Fiscal policy, on the other hand, continues to aim to stimulate demand through pump-priming without necessarily crowding out private investment.
label: positive

sentence: Recent Economic Results This overall approach produced very good results in 1999, with signs of returning stability and a reviving economy.
label: positive

sentence: Our first three quarter data show that growth has indeed rebounded as agriculture continued to perform strongly and manufacturing made a turnaround.
label: positive

sentence: Inflation was mostly tame because of ample food supply, and the ten-month average inflation rate of 7.2 percent is quite close to the full-year target of 7 percent.
label: positive

sentence: The foreign exchange market was relatively stable in the first three quarters thanks to strong capital inflows.
label: positive

sentence: Bank lending has improved, with a smaller rate of decline in September, and domestic demand strengthened, based on data on imports, car sales and power consumption.
label: positive

sentence: The recovery was due partly to the accommodative policy adopted in the first semester, with measures to help bring down interest rates combined with fiscal pump-priming to stimulate demand.
label: positive

sentence: Monetary stance was afforded greater flexibility because of the subdued inflation trend and the stable foreign exchange market.
label: positive

sentence: Actual monetary growth was also significantly below our target ceilings.
label: positive

sentence: In a word, conditions were suitable for an easier stance of monetary policy.
label: positive

sentence: In the first seven months of 1999, accommodative monetary policy brought down the Reverse Repurchase (RRP) rate to 9 percent in a series of 20 policy rate cuts.
label: positive

sentence: The reserve requirement for banks was also cut by a total of 5 percentage points to 12 percent.
label: positive

sentence: These measures enabled a decline in both the 91-day Treasury bill rate and bank lending rates.
label: positive

sentence: As a precautionary measure, the Bangko Sentral also kept base money and reserve money comfortably below the IMF program ceilings.
label: positive

sentence: Meanwhile, expansionary fiscal policy took the form of a P22-billion fiscal stimulus package featured under the 1999 budget.
label: positive

sentence: The package set aside some P14 billion for quick-gestating infrastructure projects and some P8 billion for the acceleration of back payments owed to government contractors.
label: positive

sentence: It was not entirely smooth sailing, however.
label: negative

sentence: Steering the economic ship through the Charybdis waters of the Asian crisis would be everything but smooth.
label: negative

sentence: Our fiscal authorities also have to deal with revenue shortfalls that threatened the fiscal targets.
label: negative

sentence: To cope with this, policymakers resorted to virtual belt-tightening by cutting down on non-essential expenditures.
label: neutral

sentence: Also, in July, in response to short-term foreign exchange volatility, the Bangko Sentral switched to a neutral monetary stance and kept policy rates steady.
label: neutral

sentence: As external factors such as the weakening Thai baht put pressure on the peso, our neutral stance allowed us to closely study the impact of accommodative policy before deciding to relax the stance further.
label: neutral

sentence: Since then, the currency market has re-stabilized, allowing us to make another 25-basis point cut in overnight RRP rates to 8.75 percent effective November 3.
label: positive

sentence: Earlier, we adopted the two-tier approach in our interest rate policy with the end result of achieving lower interest payments of the Bangko Sentral to the banks.
label: positive

sentence: Our aim is to induce banks to lend out their excess liquid funds, instead of parking them in the overnight window of the Bangko Sentral.
label: positive

sentence: In this way we hope to sustain the improvement in credit growth.
label: positive

sentence: Given these recent economic developments and expected turn of events for the rest of the year and the next, we believe that at this point, our banks, while watching their bottomlines, would need to be more aggressive to protect their present market share of the credit market.
label: positive

sentence: We have reached the lowest point of the economic cycle, and the only way for the various indicators of demand is up.
label: neutral

sentence: The banks, coming from the supply side, should therefore assume the leadership in optimism.
label: neutral

sentence: It is up to them to break the economic stalemate.
label: neutral

sentence: We are, therefore, encouraged by the trend in credit growth.
label: positive

sentence: More and more banks have started to open their doors to borrowers and investors.
label: positive

sentence: In due time, we should see the fruits of this positive trend.
label: positive

sentence: PRESENT AND FUTURE POLICY STANCE The question now is, what is our present stance?
label: neutral

sentence: And looking ahead, do we need to modify our existing one?
label: neutral

sentence: To begin with, policy actions in the near future -- such as the decision to further ease the monetary stance -- will indeed depend on the final shape of the economic outlook and on prevailing market conditions.
label: neutral

sentence: For this year, we are targeting an average inflation rate of 7 percent and a GNP growth rate of 3.5 to 4 percent.
label: neutral

sentence: Both goals are well within reach.
label: positive

sentence: In 2000, we expect economic conditions to improve further as the recovery gets underway.
label: positive

sentence: GDP growth is expected to be stronger, reaching 4 to 5 percent for the year.
label: positive

sentence: Improved agriculture performance and a stable foreign exchange market will help temper inflation in 2000 to an average of 6 to 7 percent.
label: positive

sentence: There are a number of challenges, of course, and further stimulation may be required to help achieve our broad economic targets.
label: neutral

sentence: Over the next few quarters, we will need to continue to nurture the industrial sector and make sure that the turnaround in manufacturing is sustained.
label: neutral

sentence: We would also like to see bank lending strengthen further and convince our local banks to gradually ease their cautious lending stance.
label: positive

sentence: And most important, we should be able to sustain our actions to intensify our reform agenda in banking, in tax administration as well as in corporate and public governance.
label: positive

sentence: The crisis has taught us to be humble for change.
label: neutral

sentence: The November rate cut reflects our belief that there is still some room for monetary easing, particularly since average inflation remains close to the year's overall target.
label: neutral

sentence: Monetary policy will continue to support growth while keeping inflation at bay, but further relaxation will be done carefully, in order to sustain the stability of the currency.
label: positive

sentence: To put it briefly, our monetary stance will remain cautious yet flexible.
label: positive

sentence: The art of monetary policy is essentially a juggling act; you try to keep several balls in the air all at once.
label: neutral

sentence: We try to balance the objective of stimulating the economy with the goals of price stability and exchange rate stability.
label: neutral

sentence: That means taking it one step at a time.
label: neutral

sentence: This means we are not targeting all monetary goals at the same time.
label: neutral

sentence: Our monetary instruments are limited.
label: neutral

sentence: Similarly, if we may speak about it, fiscal policy is also trying to balance the need to stimulate private demand with the goal of fiscal prudence.
label: neutral

sentence: Hence the fiscal stimulus package was combined with some belt-tightening.
label: neutral

sentence: Unfortunately, the target budget deficit of P85.3 billion this year may still be exceeded.
label: negative

sentence: Personally, however, I don't think that should be the main concern.
label: neutral

sentence: To my mind, at this early stage of economic recovery, we can afford to accommodate some deficits, at least temporarily, if it would also mean having increased economic activity.
label: neutral

sentence: At any rate, fiscal pressures may begin to ease soon.
label: positive

sentence: Revenue collection is expected to improve as economic growth resumes normal levels.
label: positive

sentence: More importantly, because of the more favorable economic scenario, there will be less need for government pump-priming.
label: positive

sentence: Therefore, fiscal policy in 2000 will be slightly less expansionary compared to this year.
label: neutral

sentence: Instead, the main focus will be on fiscal consolidation.
label: neutral

sentence: As the economy continues to recover, authorities need to focus on sustainability in the long haul.
label: positive

sentence: For the fiscal sector, the main concern will be balancing the budget in the medium term, say within the next two or three years.
label: neutral

sentence: There are two key strategies to do this.
label: neutral

sentence: First is strengthening the revenue base by improving tax administration.
label: neutral

sentence: Higher revenues will reduce the deficit and help pay for priority projects and programs.
label: positive

sentence: The fiscal authorities also plan to rationalize the investment incentive structure by narrowing down the list of tax and duty exemptions.
label: neutral

sentence: The second fiscal strategy is expenditure control, or cutting down on non-essential spending.
label: neutral

sentence: With these measures, authorities hope to narrow down the deficit to P62.5 billion in 2000 and hopefully reduce it to zero later on.

sentence: Ladies and gentlemen, I took the liberty of venturing onto fiscal ground.
label: neutral

sentence: I wanted to put across at this important gathering the crucial role of a healthy fiscal sector to the conduct of an independent monetary policy.
label: neutral

sentence: To the extent that the fiscal balance is in good shape, the Bangko Sentral can continue focusing on its primary mandate of keeping inflation low and the banking system robust.
label: positive

sentence: These are the basic elements of vibrant economic growth.
label: positive

sentence: Short of this scenario, however, we might also have to watch for a possible current account deficit and a weak currency.
label: negative

sentence: The curse of high interest rates could very well be again on our laundry list of priority concerns.
label: negative

sentence: At this point, we have fundamentally put this behind us.
label: neutral

sentence: And the challenge therefore is to ensure that it stays behind us as we strengthen our path to economic recovery.

sentence: THE CHALLENGES AHEAD Part of our job as policymakers is anticipating and responding to emerging issues on both the domestic and external front.
label: neutral

sentence: On the home front, the major concern will be the potential inflationary pressure from rising oil prices and adjustments in wages and transport costs.
label: negative

sentence: Here there should be less concern since we expect that strong agricultural output will help keep down prices for the basic food items, which comprise about 55 percent of the CPI.
label: positive

sentence: This was what happened in the October inflation data.

sentence: More important, greater competition is expected to water down the inflationary impact of higher petroleum prices, transport and wage adjustments.

label: negative

sentence: The main lesson from these challenges that I have outlined to you is the importance of prudent but forward-looking policy that keeps a sharp eye on new developments.
label: neutral

sentence: Monetary policy will continue to be cautious yet flexible, and fiscal policy, as we read it, will focus on consolidation.
label: neutral

sentence: The basic overall thrust is the same: we will try to nurse the recovery but preserve stability and guard against potential shocks to the economy.
label: positive

sentence: In the old days when trains ran on steam, the train engineer kept the train running smoothly by putting coal into the boiler.
label: neutral

sentence: We have already managed to get the train going, and it is running steadily.
label: positive

sentence: We have done our part and will continue to do so.
label: positive

sentence: And let me add that I hope that you will come on board and join us as we push full steam ahead into the new millennium.
label: positive

sentence: And I assure you, the coalfed locomotive is only metaphorical; when you board the Philippine train, it will run on our people's ingenuity and productivity and global competitiveness.
label: neutral

sentence: Thank you and good afternoon.

sentence: INTRODUCTION Officers and members of the Money Market Association of the Philippines, the Foreign Exchange Association of the Philippines, and the Trust Officers Association of the Philippines, distinguished guests, friends, ladies and gentlemen, good evening.

sentence: This gathering thus bespeaks good planning.

sentence: Given this propitious start and the impressive roster of participants, this evening's activity should be a stimulating and fruitful undertaking.

sentence: Developments since July 1997 have brought to the fore the importance of a stable currency market in the efficient functioning of the economy on the path to sustained growth.

sentence: RECENT ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENTS The country's recent economic performance bodes well for its prospects in the near term.

sentence: During the first half of this year, output performance was better than expected, with the real gross national product (GNP) expanding by 2.9 percent.

label: positive

sentence: Moreover, the contraction in industry appears to have been arrested, with manufacturing in particular showing signs of resurgence.

sentence: The improving economies of Japan and Europe as well as the recovery of many of the crisis-afflicted emerging countries in Asia should impact favorably on our growth prospects, even if the much-hailed strengthening of the U.S. economy should simmer down.

sentence: Consumer prices have remained broadly stable, with the inflation rate for October settling at 5.4 percent, for an average of 7.2 percent during the first ten months of the year.

sentence: MONETARY AND FINANCIAL SECTOR POLICIES In terms of our monetary policy stance, we will continue to promote growth in a low-inflation environment through the reduction in interest rates to low but sustainable levels.

sentence: The sharp decline in the industry's non-performing loans bolstered overall asset quality.

sentence: This significant improvement in banks' asset quality is expected to continue in the coming months as banks build up further their loan books and as bad loans are worked out.

sentence: The reform efforts included, among others, measures to improve the quality of bank management, increase capital requirements, improve bank asset quality, enhance transparency in the system, and align prudential rules with international norms.

sentence: Introduction I am very pleased and honored to again have this opportunity to provide you a briefing on the current economic situation and prospects.
label: neutral

sentence: Since my last briefing here at this same venue approximately a year ago, so many important developments have occurred.
label: neutral

sentence: The picture has increasingly clarified and I am happy to report to you that our fundamental conviction, that our economy will survive the Asian crisis in relatively good shape, is being validated by events.
label: positive

sentence: Main Developments After a setback in 1998, the Philippine economy is now beginning to show signs of recovery.
label: positive

sentence: GDP grew by 1.2 percent and GNP by 2.0 percent in the first quarter of 1999 after 3 successive quarters of retreat.
label: positive

sentence: Palay and corn production is staging a strong double-digit growth.
label: positive

sentence: Imports finally started to rebound in March 1999 registering a positive 1.5 percent growth.
label: positive

sentence: The month-to-month decline in bank credits to the private sector slowed down to -0.59 percent in March 1999 from a peak of -3.17 percent in January 1999.
label: positive

sentence: Unemployment declined to 9.0 percent in the first quarter of 1999 compared with 9.6 percent in the previous quarter.
label: positive

sentence: The stock market has staged a major recovery from a composite index low of 1,192 in August 1998 to the current 2,400 level.
label: positive

sentence: In a recent speech at a regional conference of central bank governors in Seoul, Korea which I attended, no less than the Managing Director of the IMF, Mr. Michel Camdessus, cited the Philippines' improving prospects.
label: positive

sentence: He said, "...The countries at the heart of the crisis are close to or even past the turning point.
label: positive

sentence: Most clearly in Korea, the Philippines, and increasingly in Thailand and Malaysia, we see signs of an upturn in activity..." On the basis of our latest projections as of April 1999 which were validated by an IMF mission, we are projecting a GDP growth of 1-3 percent and GNP growth of 1.5-3.5 percent in 1999.
label: positive

sentence: Moreover, I am more and more convinced we'll actually be closer to the top end of the forecast range.
label: positive

sentence: We have also made steady and significant gains towards stabilizing prices and removing the pressure on our external financial position.
label: positive

sentence: Price stability has been primarily sustained through careful liquidity management.
label: positive

sentence: The average inflation in 1998 of 9.0 percent was even lower than the target range of 9.25 to 9.75 percent.
label: positive

sentence: Moreover, from 11.5 percent in January 1999, inflation has slowed down to 8.0 percent as of April 1999.
label: positive

sentence: We are expecting inflation to come down to 7.5 percent by December this year.
label: positive

sentence: For the year as a whole, we are projecting an average inflation of 8-9 percent.
label: positive

sentence: Meanwhile, the current account of the balance of payments improved dramatically from a deficit of $4.4 billion in 1997 to a surplus of $1.3 billion in 1998.
label: positive

sentence: As of January 1999, even the merchandise trade deficit has turned into a $183 million surplus.
label: positive

sentence: The current account surplus reached $570 million and the overall balance of payments recorded an $881 million surplus.
label: positive

sentence: Gross international reserves have now reached an all-time high of $13.9 billion as of mid-May.
label: positive

sentence: This represents a big recovery from less than $8.8 billion as of the start of 1998.
label: positive

sentence: Due to the strong early results, we have increased our target current account surplus for 1999 from $444 million to $1.7 billion.
label: positive

sentence: The target balance of payments surplus was also increased from $1.6 billion to $3.2 billion.
label: positive

sentence: Moreover, the improvement in the balance of payments has been consistent with a manageable external debt situation.
label: positive

sentence: Only 15.0 percent of our total foreign debt of $47.8 billion as of end-December 1998 is short-term.

sentence: Our debt service ratio, or the ratio of foreign debt service payments to total exports of goods and services, has actually declined from 17.1 percent in 1993 to 11.7 percent in 1998.
label: positive

sentence: This used to be more than 36 percent in the mid-1980s.
label: neutral

sentence: The improvements in the external transactions have led to the recovery of the peso to the current level of around P38 to the dollar.
label: positive

sentence: Moreover, there is now also more normal movement of the peso in the context of a floating exchange rate policy.
label: positive

sentence: The Monetary Policy Stance The recent positive developments especially on inflation and in the foreign exchange market have made possible the significant lowering of interest rates that will support economic recovery.
label: positive

sentence: Since the start of the year, the BSP has already cut its market-setting overnight borrowing and lending rates no less than 15 times resulting in a cumulative reduction totaling 350 basis points as of May 24, 1999.
label: positive

sentence: We have also cut the liquidity reserve requirement by a total three percentage points this year.
label: positive

sentence: With the statutory reserve requirement at 10 percent and the liquidity reserve at 4 percent, the overall reserve requirement is now down to only 14 percent which is even lower than the pre-crisis level of 15 percent (13 percent statutory, 2 percent liquidity).
label: positive

sentence: Reflecting the easing of liquidity, the market benchmark 91-day bill rate has dropped to single-digit at 9.741 percent as of the May 31 auction.
label: positive

sentence: Meanwhile, bank prime lending rates have eased to around 12-13 percent.
label: positive

sentence: Bank lending spreads have also come down to 3-4 percent.
label: positive

sentence: We are anticipating that the lower and more reasonable interest rate environment will help in sustaining economic recovery.
label: positive

sentence: An Update on the Banking System One of the key reasons why the Philippines is able to lead the way to economic recovery in crisis-stricken Asia is the resiliency and relative soundness of the banking system.
label: positive

sentence: Bank failures have continued to be very limited.
label: positive

sentence: The total assets of failed banks, including Orient Bank, account for less than one-third of one percent of total banking system assets.
label: positive

sentence: As a result, it has not been necessary to commit massive amounts of public funds just to bail-out the banking system.
label: neutral

sentence: In sharp contrast, the fiscal costs of bank rehabilitation in other countries have been staggering.
label: negative

sentence: For example, very recently we were informed by Korean authorities that they are now projecting to spend about $54 billion purely for cleaning up the balance sheets of their surviving banking system.
label: negative

sentence: In Thailand, the Governor of the Bank of Thailand told me they have already spent about $22 billion and still counting.
label: negative

sentence: Looking forward, I am confident that our banking system will continue to remain viable and resilient especially with economic recovery in clear prospect.
label: positive

sentence: Asset quality, as revealed in the NPL ratio is respectable.
label: positive

sentence: The NPL ratio of the commercial banking system, which accounts for about 91 percent of total banking system assets, was estimated at 13.18 percent as of March 1999.
label: neutral

sentence: In comparison, private sector estimates of NPL ratios in other severely affected countries are much higher, ranging from 30 percent in Korea to about 75 percent in Indonesia.
label: neutral

sentence: Capital adequacy also remains at a very comfortable level.
label: positive

sentence: At 17.7 percent as of the latest report, the ratio of net worth to risk assets is presently among the highest in the region.
label: positive

sentence: Finally, and more importantly, the future stability of the banking system is being secured with the vigorous implementation of banking reforms.
label: positive

sentence: The key measures have been increased minimum capitalization requirements, higher loan loss requirements and their stricter enforcement, and strengthened banking supervision.
label: positive

sentence: In response to the banking reforms as well as the competitive environment, we are already witnessing a market-driven, voluntary, and orderly consolidation in the banking industry.
label: positive

sentence: In the long-run, this is for the better and will result in a stronger banking system.
label: positive

sentence: Is the Worst Really Over?
label: neutral

sentence: Is the worst really over?
label: neutral

sentence: Summing up the overall picture, I believe the evidence is quite clear that we have already regained a reasonable degree of financial stability.
label: positive

sentence: This stability has given us the solid foundation to pursue our economic growth objectives.
label: positive

sentence: I believe the worst is behind us.
label: positive

sentence: However, realistically speaking, we must also admit that significant challenges remain.
label: negative

sentence: The prospects could be particularly affected by external events that have a domestic impact.
label: neutral

sentence: Some of the key challenges that we have to look out for include: (1) the ability of the high-flying US economy to achieve a soft landing that can ensure the sustainable growth of the U.S. and world economies; (2) the ability of the Japanese economy to finally turn around; (3) fresh crisis in major developing economies; (4) a major adjustment in the Chinese currency; and (5) major disasters and weather-related disturbances.
label: negative

sentence: On the U.S. economy, it is already widely expected that the U.S. economy will have to slow down and correct.
label: negative

sentence: In fact, our own projections anticipate this eventuality.

label: neutral

sentence: On the Japanese economy, the authorities there have been taking major initiatives both to stimulate the economy and to clean up the banking system.
label: neutral

sentence: There is therefore reasonable basis to expect progress.
label: neutral

sentence: On the other hand, the chances of a fresh crisis in key markets have also been significantly reduced in view of strong actions being taken by the most vulnerable countries like Brazil and Argentina.
label: neutral

sentence: Even in Russia, the policy dialogue appears to be moving forward according to the IMF.
label: neutral

sentence: We are also not expecting a major adjustment in the Chinese currency in the near future.
label: neutral

label: positive

sentence: These are the key risks that we have to face in the period ahead but I believe, on balance, the probability of more success rather than reversal is sufficiently encouraging.
label: neutral

sentence: Concluding Remarks I would like to finish my presentation today with an update on the financial condition of the BSP.
label: neutral

sentence: The agenda, as you will learn quickly enough, is not a mechanical duplication of the international reforms.
label: neutral

sentence: Under the forthcoming new arrangements, which have actually started to be implemented, compliance with the risk-based minimum capital adequacy ratio reinforced by more disciplined and fair asset valuations will be critical.
label: neutral

sentence: No less important is the necessity of having more responsible bank governance starting with qualified, fully enabled and vigilant boards of directors.
label: neutral

sentence: The General Banking Law has several important provisions that support this including the requirement of having at least two independent directors, the adoption of the fit and proper rule that will allow the board to disqualify bank directors and officers when warranted, and stricter provisions against self-dealing.
label: neutral

sentence: A key General Banking Law reform that should also be fully supported is the call for more disclosure to the public to promote transparency.
label: neutral

sentence: Already, the Bangko Sentral has taken important steps to implement this through new regulations.
label: positive

sentence: But it would be more ideal if all banks go beyond minimum compliance and truly welcome the opportunity to keep its stakeholders well-informed.
label: positive

sentence: This is a sure way to gain the public trust.
label: positive

sentence: I cannot overemphasize enough the first and foremost need to mind the safety and soundness of our banks.
label: negative

sentence: Without this, our banking system would indeed be built upon shaky foundations.
label: negative

sentence: But the challenges of the time also call upon all of us to continue innovating as well.
label: positive

sentence: I strongly believe that it will be a fatal error to assume that rural banking is essentially a low technology business.
label: negative

sentence: It is easy enough to slide into that assumption considering the setting.
label: neutral

sentence: Perhaps that's the reason why the rural banking system has yet to fully meet its potential and promise.
label: negative

sentence: But I am also encouraged to note that there is an emerging generation of new rural bankers, thoroughly modern, energetic, and above all open to examining new credit and management paradigms.
label: positive

sentence: They are being supported by enlightened donors and researchers who are bringing to bear their considerable resources, field experience and intellectual capital to deal with long-standing problems that have perpetuated poverty in the countryside.
label: positive

sentence: I am informed that some of your very worthy cooperative projects include the development of a computer-based management information system that will be widely available to our rural banks by early next year.
label: positive

sentence: Through enhanced use of appropriate information technology, our banks should be better able to handle the intensive information requirements of micro-lending from origination to collection.
label: positive

sentence: Hopefully, banks will also be able to meet our information requirements to ensure more effective and proactive supervision as well as better policy analysis.
label: positive

sentence: In this regard, we have announced new reporting arrangements under BSP Circular 249.
label: neutral

sentence: We realize that the transition to the new reporting arrangements geared towards paperless reporting may not be that easy given the limitations in a significant number of our rural banks.
label: negative

sentence: Nevertheless, we need to take right away the first steps if ever we are to complete a potentially long journey.
label: neutral

sentence: Depending on our assessment of compliance, we would be willing to consider some flexibility in the implementation timetable if warranted.
label: neutral

sentence: Rest assured that we will be coordinating closely with the association on this matter as well as in other regulations that concern the industry.
label: positive

sentence: I am also aware of your efforts to adopt new credit delivery technologies that will give traditionally non-bankable clients greater access to credit without necessarily sacrificing credit quality while encouraging them to save as well.
label: positive

sentence: By moving in these directions, I have no doubt in my mind that the rural banking system will continue to find an important and strategic niche in our financial system.
label: positive

sentence: The policy of the Bangko Sentral is to promote a strong and capable banking system and not necessarily to create large banks and eliminate small players.
label: neutral

sentence: Let me also assure you that we too as a regulator are changing our ways to ensure that we keep pace with market-driven innovations.
label: positive

sentence: We would like to be perceived as a regulator that adds value by promoting public confidence without necessarily stifling progress.
label: positive

sentence: Concluding note In closing, I would like to emphasize that the Bangko Sentral intends to continue with banking reform efforts with its priorities embodied in the new General Banking Law and the proposed amendments to the New Central Bank Act.
label: positive

sentence: Overall, these legislative changes aim to further strengthen our banking system, raise prudential standards to international norms, foster greater competition and enhance the supervisory capability and independence of the Bangko Sentral.
label: positive

sentence: Some have voiced concern that the beneficial provision of the General Banking Law may not accrue to the rural banking industry due to conflict with provisions of the Rural Banks Act.
label: negative

sentence: I don't believe there are a lot of confilicts in the first place, nevertheless, I wish to inform you that we are now working on the implementing regulations that will rationalize potentially conflicting provisions of different operative laws.
label: positive

sentence: In this regard, we will be generally adopting a liberal interpretation in favor of rural banks in resolving such conflicts.
label: positive

sentence: Echoing the president, I am strongly urging the leaders and members of the association "to continue working together with the government and maintain our sights on the endless possibilities for the nation's growth."
label: positive

sentence: I now wish all of you a fruitful and mutually rewarding symposium.

sentence: Thank you very much and a pleasant day to all.
