sentence: Secretary Camacho, Secretary Perez, distinguished members of the Singaporean Financial Community, honored guests, ladies and gentlemen, good afternoon.
label: neutral

sentence: As you have heard from Secretary Camacho, the Philippines has an economic plan focused on fiscal discipline, good governance, market-oriented reform, and poverty-reduction.
label: neutral

sentence: As the Governor of the Central Bank, I would like to reiterate my support for this plan and its implementation and also my confidence that there is a team and a strategy in place to ensure that the objectives included in the economic plan will be achieved.
label: positive

sentence: Allow me, therefore, to spell out the initiatives that we, at the Central Bank, have been taking to support the Government's economic plan.
label: neutral

sentence: Our efforts have been directed in three important areas: first, achieving price stability ; second, introducing banking sector reforms ; and third, introducing institutional reforms to pave the way for the good governance practices that will assist in making the Philippine economic plan a reality.
label: positive

sentence: Price Stability To my mind, one of the most crucial steps being taken by the Arroyo Administration is the emphasis on instilling fiscal discipline throughout the public sector.
label: positive

sentence: The ability to meet revenue and expenditure targets, and thus reduce the budget deficit, are significant challenges that must be accomplished.
label: neutral

sentence: While also addressing rampant speculative activity in some quarters of the financial markets.
label: negative

sentence: While the Government is focused on instilling fiscal discipline, my team in the Central Bank has also been concentrating on restoring calm to the currency markets.
label: positive

sentence: I am pleased to say that we have seen some initial success in this very important area.
label: positive

sentence: Our goal in this regard is to achieve overall price stability and to continue to support the Government in meeting its fiscal targets.
label: positive

sentence: As many of you are aware, our currency, the peso, like many other currencies around the world, has been the object of some speculative pressure.
label: negative

sentence: In addressing this pressure, we have taken a two-fold approach to our currency challenges by reducing liquidity levels in the market through hikes in liquidity reserve requirements, and by being diligent and relentless in warding off speculators.
label: positive

sentence: We have done this by exposing banks and by imposing fines when they are found to have violated foreign exchange regulations.
label: neutral

sentence: Our efforts have resulted in a strengthening of the peso from a low of PhP 53.57 to the U.S. dollar three weeks ago to its current level of close to PhP 51.
label: positive

sentence: A relatively stable exchange rate is important for keeping inflation under control and is good for business planning.
label: neutral

sentence: Our next step is to ensure that price stability becomes the norm rather than the exception.
label: neutral

sentence: To achieve this, the Central Bank will be implementing a number of institutional measures.
label: neutral

sentence: Those measures include: A shift in monetary policy to inflation-targeting by January of next year.
label: neutral

sentence: This practice, which is employed by many other countries, has price stability as a primary focus.
label: positive

sentence: It achieves that stability by committing to reach an explicit nominal inflation target directly without compromising the broad economic targets of the government.
label: positive

sentence: This differs from the present practice of using a monetary aggregate targeting framework, which uses money supply as an intermediate target.
label: neutral

sentence: Other institutional measures that we are taking include the preservation of an appropriate interest-rate differential to encourage investors to maintain their peso denominated assets.
label: neutral

sentence: They also include the maintenance of a market-determined exchange rate policy.
label: neutral

sentence: This policy, however, must be supported by a strict and effective foreign exchange monitoring system and a strong commitment to apply penalties to institutions or individuals who violate foreign exchange regulations.
label: neutral

sentence: And finally, our efforts to support price stability include work we are carrying out to develop secondary markets for fixed income and derivative products.
label: positive

sentence: Instituting Banking Reforms The Central Bank is also committed to increasing foreign participation in the Philippine banking sector.
label: positive

sentence: As such, we will soon introduce reforms to prepare domestic banks to effectively compete in a globally integrated banking environment.
label: positive

sentence: The reforms will fall within the framework of first, improving the banking sector's structure; secondly, enhancing the Central Bank's supervisory and regulatory powers in line with international standards; thirdly, heightening bank transparency and disclosure; fourthly, improving risk management; and finally, stimulating the consumer-oriented credit environment.
label: positive

sentence: Our work to strengthen the banking sector through such steps as imposing capital requirements in accordance with international standards and by promoting greater competition will certainly encourage banking sector consolidation and provide opportunities for greater foreign participation in the sector.
label: positive

sentence: In enhancing our supervisory powers, the Central Bank has introduced risk-based capital requirements, tighter loan classification and provisioning rules, and consolidated reporting.
label: positive

sentence: We will also be imposing stiffer penalties for any violations of the Philippines banking regulations.
label: neutral

sentence: In heightening bank transparency and disclosure, the Central Bank will monitor the banks' selection of external auditors and require strict adherence to the "Know your customer rule".
label: neutral

sentence: We will also require the quarterly publication of statements of condition, key financial indicators, non-performing loans, and related-party lending.
label: neutral

sentence: In improving risk management, the Central Bank will institute an early warning system for bank failures, assist banks to better match their foreign currency assets and liabilities, and carry out reviews to identify regulatory loopholes in the transactions of bank-affiliated FOREX corporations.
label: positive

sentence: In an effort to stimulate bank lending, the Central Bank will encourage the financing of micro-enterprises by providing incentives such as liquidity assistance through re-discounting.
label: positive

sentence: We will also relax certain collateralization requirements and partially lift the general moratorium on the licensing of new thrift and rural banks to allow the establishment of institutions who are committed to micro-enterprise financing.
label: positive

sentence: Institutional Reforms and Good Governance These reforms, if they are to have the desired impact, must be complemented by both good governance practices and strong political will.
label: neutral

sentence: For this reason, we are seeking amendments to the Central Bank Charter that will institutionalize many of these reforms and reinforce the position of the central bank as a governing and independent body for monetary policy and supervision.
label: positive

sentence: In considering amendments to the Central Bank Charter, we are looking to add criteria to close or placing banks or other financial institutions under receivership in line with international standards.
label: neutral

sentence: We also want to strengthen criminal and administrative penalties for any violation of banking laws, and to introduce measures that will increase the Central Bank's operational efficiency.
label: positive

sentence: Finally, I am very optimistic that we will soon see the passage of an Anti-money Laundering Bill in both the Philippine Congress and Senate.
label: positive

sentence: This is something that the Central Bank has been working on over the past several months.
label: neutral

sentence: I would like to assure you that this bill is being taken very seriously and has gained popular support not just in the legislative arena, but also amongst the general public.
label: positive

sentence: Let me make it perfectly clear, the Central Bank is committed to promoting and enforcing international standards of banking regulation and supervision.
label: positive

sentence: Because of this, we have zero tolerance for illegal practices such as money laundering.
label: positive

sentence: These are not empty words.
label: neutral

sentence: My Central Bank team and my colleagues in government will be steadfast in instituting the reforms that will strengthen our banking sector and promote good governance.
label: positive

sentence: My former associates and colleagues in the Philippine banking sector can certainly attest to my determination to work with the Government to achieve its growth targets.
label: positive

sentence: And while I am holding the reins at the Central Bank, good governance policies and practices will be non-negotiable.
label: positive

sentence: Before I close and take your questions, I would just like to reiterate that we recognize that restoring the Philippine economy to health is a significant challenge.
label: neutral

sentence: But, I am very optimistic that this challenge will be met because we are approaching this task with a clear plan, a team of experienced professionals who are managing this process and a commitment throughout President Arroyo's administration to ensure that the Philippine's path to prosperity is based on a strong foundation of economic reform.
label: positive

sentence: Thank you very much and good day.
label: neutral

sentence: (Note: Luncheon meeting with the Singaporean Financial Community, 24 August 2001, Shargri-La Hotel, Singapore)
label: neutral

sentence: Mr. Edgardo E. Tumangan, officers and members of the Rotary Club of Manila, friends, ladies and gentlemen, good afternoon: I.
label: neutral

sentence: Introduction It is a pleasure to join you again at this membership meeting of the Rotary Club of Manila.
label: neutral

sentence: Occasions such as this one bring to mind the importance of a dynamic government-private sector partnership in confronting the challenges we face today.
label: neutral

sentence: I believe that, now more than ever, the role of the private sector--in particular, the business sector as represented by groups such as the Rotary Club--is crucial and indispensable in the effort to sustain the economy's growth momentum.
label: positive

sentence: Given this, the continuing challenge for government, and the BSP in particular, is in providing a sound macroeconomic environment to support economic activity.
label: neutral

sentence: The BSP is specifically tasked to provide direction in the areas of monetary policy and banking.
label: neutral

sentence: Allow me to discuss some of these issues with you today.
label: neutral

sentence: II.
label: neutral

sentence: Recent Policy Initiatives The BSP's efforts to ensure a supportive environment are centered on its primary mandate of maintaining stable prices, while broadly supporting the economy's output growth objective.
label: neutral

sentence: We did this in the early part of the 2001 by reducing policy interest rates consistent with an accommodative stance of monetary policy.
label: neutral

sentence: BSP policy rates were reduced from the beginning of the year by a total of 450 basis points to provide room for market interest rates to go down and thereby encourage economic activity to strengthen.
label: positive

sentence: This was in addition to the earlier reduction of 150 basis points in BSP policy rates in December 2000.
label: neutral

sentence: In recent months, however, the movements in international oil prices and the peso-dollar rate have posed upside risks to the inflation outlook.
label: negative

sentence: These factors have prompted us to reassess our monetary policy stance and focus on addressing potential inflationary pressures.
label: neutral

sentence: The stability of the foreign exchange market is of particular concern because sharp and sustained movements in the peso-dollar rate have a pass-through impact on consumer prices by raising the cost of imported inputs for production.
label: negative

sentence: To address potential inflationary pressures, the BSP recently increased its liquidity reserve requirement by 2 percentage points.
label: positive

sentence: This was aimed at moderating liquidity growth and, at the same time, helping temper speculative activity in the foreign exchange market.
label: positive

sentence: There are indications that part of the foreign exchange market activities in recent months have been funded by the excess peso liquidity in the system.
label: negative

sentence: Tempering liquidity growth will reduce the potential for speculation in the foreign exchange market.
label: positive

sentence: It will also bring overall monetary growth in line with the downwardly revised growth targets for 2001 based on the weaker-than-expected upturn in economic activity.
label: neutral

sentence: We have implemented other policy measures to further ease pressures on the peso.
label: positive

sentence: The BSP expanded the Currency Risk Protection Program (CRPP) by providing a hedging mechanism to a greater number of users, specifically market players wanting to frontload their future foreign currency requirements and borrowers wanting to cover unmatured foreign currency obligations.
label: positive

sentence: We tightened monitoring of foreign exchange transactions of banks and reduced the ceilings on undocumented over-the-counter foreign exchange sales.
label: positive

sentence: We also did away with the 3-tier pricing mechanism for the placement of overnight funds by banks with the BSP.
label: neutral

sentence: Some help came from the private sector as well.
label: positive

sentence: Notably, member banks of the Bankers' Association of the Philippines (BAP) agreed to voluntarily reduce their overbought foreign exchange positions so as to help improve foreign exchange supply.
label: positive

sentence: With the measures that we have implemented, the exchange rate has stabilized, closing at P53.15 yesterday.
label: positive

sentence: Promoting a stronger domestic banking system remains another key priority for the Bangko Sentral.
label: neutral

sentence: We believe that a healthy banking and financial system will be better equipped to provide for the financial intermediation needs of our domestic industries.
label: positive

sentence: To this end, the BSP implemented a host of policy changes involving the banking system.
label: neutral

sentence: For example, we issued various regulations to implement the General Banking Law of 2000, the basic legal fabric governing the entire banking system.
label: neutral

sentence: Various circulars were issued by the BSP in 2001 on the following: Definition and classification of banks and the prerequisites for the grant of universal banking authorities; Guidelines on microfinance loans to small and medium enterprises; Guidelines for the new risk-based capital adequacy requirement, which took effect on 1 July 2001.
label: neutral

sentence: Meanwhile, the BSP continues to be at the forefront in pushing for the legislation against money laundering to protect the integrity of our financial institutions.
label: positive

sentence: Thus, the BSP intends to push for the passage in congress of a comprehensive Anti-money Laundering Law, as a key legislative priority.
label: positive

sentence: We take heart that the president in her State of the Nation Address has certified as urgent this law, which incorporates all the basic elements of an anti-money laundering system covering the proceeds of crimes including plunder, graft and corruption, illegal drugs, smuggling, kidnapping, as well as financial and tax fraud.
label: positive

sentence: The reforms introduced in the General Banking Law of 2000 will be complemented by the proposed amendments to the BSP Charter.
label: positive

sentence: These amendments aim to enhance the BSP's supervisory powers, improve prudential standards, and promote greater banking competition.
label: positive

sentence: Key reforms include the grant of authority to the BSP to conduct more frequent banking examinations, restoration of the BSP's tax-exempt status with respect to open market operations, and the enhancement of the scope and strength of administrative sanctions.
label: neutral

sentence: These proposed changes will lead to what we call a "safer financial architecture" inasmuch as they help fortify our banking system against various potential shocks.
label: positive

sentence: III.
label: neutral

sentence: Key Economic Results The measures I have outlined to you have contributed over the last few months to a more favorable economic environment and a more sustainable path to long-term growth.
label: positive

sentence: For example, inflation has been kept in check in the past few months.
label: positive

sentence: Actual inflation continues to be on track relative to its projected path for 2001, averaging at 6.8 percent in the first seven months.
label: positive

sentence: Latest readings on the inflation environment are consistent with our expectations that the inflation target of 6.0-7.0 percent for the year will be met--although perhaps the actual value will be closer to the higher end of the range.
label: positive

sentence: At the same time, our financial system continues to be for the most part stable and sound.
label: positive

sentence: Total assets continue to expand, with the capital-to-risk assets ratio at 16.95 percent as of April 2001 compared to 16.21 percent in December 2000.
label: positive

sentence: Asset quality has remained relatively manageable, with the non-performing loan ratio for commercial banks settling at 16.81 percent as of May 2001.
label: neutral

sentence: Meanwhile, our accommodative monetary stance earlier in the year has led to a general downtrend in market interest rates.
label: positive

sentence: The 91-day treasury bill rate, for example, fell from a high of 16.72 percent in November 2000, when political tensions were running high, to 9.22 percent at the latest auction on 30 July 2001.
label: positive

sentence: Similarly, banks' lending rates declined to a range of 12.62-14.25 percent for the period 26 July - 1 Aug 2001 compared to 18.3-20.2 percent in the first week of December.
label: positive

sentence: As a result, domestic credit continued to expand, rising by 10.08 percent in May with higher loans extended to both the public and private sectors.
label: positive

sentence: The resulting expansion in credit has provided the basis for moderate economic growth in the face of domestic concerns and weak external demand.
label: positive

sentence: Real GNP grew by 3.6 percent in the first quarter of 2001, underpinned by personal consumption and government spending on the demand side, and by the agriculture and services sectors on the supply side.
label: positive

sentence: The resiliency of these productive sectors will provide a steadying influence for economic growth in the face of the ongoing global economic slowdown.
label: positive

sentence: IV.
label: neutral

sentence: Concluding Remarks Despite our progress in pushing the economy further along the path to long-term sustainable growth, much still needs to be done.
label: negative

sentence: Uncertainties stemming mainly from external developments are present.
label: negative

sentence: The particular concern is how to deal with the ongoing slowdown in global economic growth.
label: neutral

sentence: The lingering weakness of the major economies such as the U.S. and Japan have affected the exports of their trading partners.
label: negative

sentence: As a result, countries like the Philippines have to rely on themselves to provide the impetus for economic growth.
label: negative

sentence: We can do this with higher consumption spending, and the key to doing this is by keeping up consumer confidence.
label: positive

sentence: This is why I spoke of the role of the private sector at the beginning of my talk.
label: neutral

sentence: At the same time, the Government, for its part, recognizes that sustained economic expansion requires the implementation of a credible and consistent policy framework, as a complement to structural reforms aimed a fostering a vibrant economy.
label: neutral

sentence: The key challenge is to accelerate the pace of the reforms in the infrastructure, financial, and agriculture sectors to boost domestic and external confidence and to enable a recovery in investments on a durable basis.
label: positive

sentence: We have weathered many difficult challenges in the past, and I have no doubt that we can get through the present problems posed by the weak global economy.
label: positive

sentence: What is different today is that there is a greater collective awareness of the need for genuine economic reforms and a greater commitment to address our problems accordingly.
label: positive

sentence: This is our key to creating the sustaining momentum for economic growth in the years ahead.
label: positive

sentence: Let us strive to preserve this commitment.
label: positive

sentence: Thank you and good afternoon.
label: neutral

sentence: (Note: Fifth Membership Meeting, Rotary Club of Manila, 09 August 3000, Manila Hotel)
label: neutral

sentence: Good afternoon, ladies and gentlemen.
label: neutral

sentence: I am pleased that you can join us today.
label: neutral

sentence: You and your businesses are important contributors to the Philippine economy and today, I want to review important initiatives that the government is taking to ensure that there is a regulatory environment to enable you and others in the private sector to carry out your business activities with certainty and transparency.
label: neutral

sentence: The Anti-money Laundering Bill, which we will review in a moment, is key to the creation of a stable and predictable environment for the private sector.
label: neutral

sentence: Central to this stability, and our economic recovery, is a strong banking system, which requires a regulatory framework in line with international banking standards.
label: neutral

sentence: Toward that end, the Central Bank is continuing to encourage bank consolidation in accordance with prudent banking standards.
label: positive

sentence: And those banking standards include addressing the global issue of money laundering.
label: neutral

sentence: As you know, our country has recently been placed on a list of Non-cooperative Countries and Territories, or NCCT, by the Financial Action Task Force, an agency created a decade ago by the G7 economic powers to co-ordinate international efforts to fight trading in questionable cash.
label: negative

sentence: To give you some background, the financial action task force originated from the G7 summit in Paris in 1989 and has the support of 29 countries, including the global financial centers of the U.S., the UK, Switzerland, Hong Kong and Singapore.
label: positive

sentence: The task force also has the support of two other international organizations, the European Commission and the Gulf Cooperation.
label: positive

sentence: Inclusion on the NCCT list is not something anyone should be proud of and it is something that is totally inconsistent with the government's effort to promote investor confidence and enhance the environment for investment.
label: negative

sentence: We have been working with the international financial community including the U.S. Treasury on this issue for the past year.
label: neutral

sentence: As the lead agency in developing the Anti-money Laundering Bill, let me say that I believe that this bill will support the maintenance and growth of foreign investment in the Philippines while protecting the rights of businesses and individuals to confidentiality.
label: positive

sentence: Without the bill, we run the risk of increasing the exposure of most, if not all, of our foreign exchange transactions to unnecessary scrutiny.
label: negative

sentence: We also run the risk that additional sanctions, together with stricter surveillance, processing and verification requirements, will be imposed - a potential disincentive for investment in, and business with, Philippine companies.
label: negative

sentence: That would be a very real consequence should the Financial Action Task Force continue to include the Philippines on the NCCT list.
label: neutral

sentence: There are also two other consequences to noncompliance that will jeopardize our efforts for economic recovery and your normal conduct of business.
label: negative

sentence: The first is adverse advisories.
label: neutral

sentence: These, if issued about the Philippines to warn international banks and other financial institutions, will create a bias, encouraging international banks to take caution when handling any Philippine-based transaction.
label: negative

sentence: This will mean that your normal business transactions may be considered or treated as possibly suspicious, in many cases discouraging foreign parties from investing or doing business with Philippine companies.
label: negative

sentence: The second consequence is an increased risk of a breach in the confidentiality of deposit accounts since a NCCT inclusion means that foreign banks can require absolute and general waivers on account ownership confidentiality before accepting transactions for processing.
label: negative

sentence: This requirement also adds to the cost of business because the additional documentation and verification associated with general waivers translates into more work and therefore more time spent in processing transactions.
label: negative

sentence: It is with these business consequences in mind, along with the spirit of international cooperation and a commitment to see the Philippines benefit from full participation in the global economy, that we are promoting the Anti-money Laundering Bill.
label: positive

sentence: Through the Anti-money Laundering Bill, the government is doing its part to create a stable and predictable environment in which you can continue to conduct your business.
label: positive

sentence: We are making this necessary reform to increase the confidence in our banking system.
label: positive

sentence: This is consistent with our commitment to economic recovery, which we believe can only take place if it is underpinned by good governance and effective institutional reform.
label: positive

sentence: I can also assure you that implementation of the Anti-money Laundering Bill will be carried out in a fair and transparent manner.
label: positive

sentence: We are a business-oriented government and we want to see you succeed and continue to contribute to the Philippine economy.
label: positive

sentence: I can also assure you that any questions that you have about the bill will be answered.
label: positive

sentence: Allow me to now turn the presentation to Atty.
label: neutral

sentence: De Zuniga who will provide you with further details and the structural framework of the bill.
label: neutral

sentence: He will be followed by a representative from the Bankers Association of the Philippines who will provide you with a private sector perspective on this bill.
label: neutral

sentence: That perspective has proved to be a valuable guideline as this bill has been developed and reviewed.
label: positive

sentence: Thank you.
label: neutral

sentence: (Note: Briefing on Anti-Money Laundering, Federation of Filipino-Chinese Chambers of Commerce & Industry, Inc., Chinese Filipino Business Club, Inc. and Filipino-Chinese General Chambers of Commerce, August 6, 2001, MB Auditorium, BSP Complex)
label: neutral

sentence: It is with great pleasure that I join this gathering of professionals and executives of the country's top corporations and other distinguished members of the Rotary Club of Makati and Rotary Club of Makati West.
label: neutral

sentence: The topic I chose for this afternoon is among the most important issues facing law enforcers and regulatory authorities.
label: neutral

sentence: Today, money laundering is already considered a global phenomenon and its detection and control has become one of the priorities of authorities.
label: negative

sentence: The use of the world's banking systems to hide and disguise funds from illegal activities can erode the integrity of financial institutions.
label: negative

sentence: It exposes society to the harmful influence of organized crime.
label: negative

sentence: As such money laundering can result in the loss of trust and confidence of domestic and foreign investors in the financial system.
label: negative

sentence: Inclusion of the Philippines in FATF list In June 2000, our country, together with 14 other nations,[1] was identified by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) as being non-cooperative in the international fight against money laundering.
label: negative

sentence: The FATF is a 29-member international group created at the 1989 G-7 Economic Summit and tasked to coordinate the drive against money laundering.
label: neutral

sentence: Our inclusion in their list was on account of what were perceived to be serious deficiencies in the country's efforts to counter money laundering.
label: negative

sentence: The Philippine's strict bank secrecy laws and the absence of legislation criminalizing money laundering activities are seen as making the country a potential haven for money laundering.
label: negative

sentence: Last 22 June 2001, the FATF decided to remove the Bahamas, Cayman Islands, Liechtenstein and Panama from the list but retained the Philippines in the updated list of non-cooperative countries.
label: neutral

sentence: The FATF has recommended the application of additional countermeasures with respect to the Philippines, Nauru and Russia by 30 September 2001 unless these countries pass the needed legislation to address identified money laundering concerns.
label: positive

sentence: These countermeasures include the possibility of enhanced surveillance and reporting of financial transactions by financial institutions whenever they transact with Philippine-based banks, businesses, and individuals.
label: neutral

sentence: [2] The adoption of these countermeasures may prove costly to the Philippines as it may hamper trade and investment transactions of domestic corporations.
label: negative

sentence: These countermeasures will generate greater scrutiny and as a result, information on transactions will unnecessarily be revealed.
label: negative

sentence: Aside from the possibility of higher transaction costs in our international dealings, the countermeasures may also give the Philippines a stigma that it is a non-cooperative country in the global fight against money laundering.
label: negative

sentence: Consequently, this may adversely affect our efforts to attract more foreign investments.
label: negative

sentence: Measures implemented The tough stance being taken by the international community towards countries with shortcomings in their efforts to curb anti-money laundering prompted the BSP, since July 2000, to immediately take strong actions.
label: positive

sentence: Last year, we issued a number of circulars that would bring the country's regulatory framework on anti-money laundering closer to international standards.
label: positive

sentence: These measures, among others, included: 1) Instructing foreign exchange corporations that are affiliates or subsidiaries of banks to report details of all foreign exchange transactions; 2) Requiring banks and non-banks to establish the true identity of their clients; 3) Requiring banks and non-banks to report suspicious transactions; 4) Prohibiting banks from issuing cashier's, manager's or certified checks payable to cash or a numbered account; and 5) Requiring foreign exchange corporations that are subsidiaries or affiliates of banks, quasi-banks and non-bank financial intermediaries to demand documents from resident buyers of foreign exchange in amounts of $10,000 or more.

sentence: A sea of change The Philippine banking system has undergone a series of reform episodes in the past.
label: neutral

sentence: These reform measures were aimed at enhancing the market-orientation of the sector and upgrading the supervisory and regulatory framework governing banks.
label: positive

sentence: The banking system is now reaping some of the "reform dividends" as it was able to withstand and recover quickly from the adverse effects of the 1997 financial crisis.
label: positive

sentence: Today, the banking system continues to be sound, showing its fundamental strength in many respects.
label: positive

sentence: Assets continue to grow to almost P3.0 trillion as of end-March 2000.
label: positive

sentence: The capital adequacy ratio of commercial banks, which at 17.24 percent as of March 2000, remains well above international standards.
label: positive

sentence: Asset quality has been manageable, with the non-performing loan ratio at 14.41 percent as of May 2000, without the use of public money to bail out distressed financial institutions.

sentence: Moreover, banks have also increased their loan loss provisioning in compliance with the higher provisioning requirements.

sentence: The current favorable state of the banking system is an indication that, indeed, the banking system has avoided the worst effects of the Asian crisis.

sentence: The past decade also saw the rapid transformation of financial markets and institutions across the globe.
label: neutral

sentence: The international financial system has grown in size, breadth and complexity as markets became more integrated and as financial instruments multiplied and became more sophisticated.
label: positive

sentence: One underlying force behind this transformation is the adoption of policy reforms in different countries that favored greater openness of domestic economy.
label: neutral

sentence: Another driving force consists of the technological advances made in the fields of telecommunications and information processing.
label: neutral

sentence: These two forces combined to substantially reduce transaction costs and build a global network of financial markets that transcends geographical boundaries, national borders and time zones.

sentence: What are the implications of this new environment to emerging economies?
label: neutral

label: negative

sentence: We do not have to look far back to recall the debilitating effects of the 1997 Asian financial crisis.

sentence: The challenge is to be able to meaningfully take part in this increasingly globalized financial community while diminishing--if not completely avoiding--the adverse impact of financial crises.
label: neutral

sentence: Responding to global opportunities and challenges A key lesson from our experiences in weathering the Asian financial crisis is that banking sector soundness and financial diversity can help limit the effects of economic disturbances.

sentence: But how do we achieve this objective of soundness and diversity in the financial sector in an increasingly globalized environment?

sentence: Upgrading supervision practices One of the priorities of the BSP is the upgrading of its supervision capability.

sentence: If the supervisory system is to remain effective in fostering the safety and soundness of the country's financial system, it must adjust to the changing structure of that system.

sentence: The on-site examination, off-site monitoring and analysis techniques of the BSP have been improved.

sentence: This is more dynamic than the purely compliance-based and checklist-driven approach.

sentence: Equally important, we have also begun implementing consolidated supervision of financial conglomerates to better assess bank risks.

sentence: Furthermore a first-generation early warning system has been started to facilitate the evaluation of bank solvency.

sentence: Upgrading prudential standards Domestic standards have been brought closer to international best practices, in such areas as capitalization, connected lending, loan concentration, loan provisioning, data disclosure and qualifications of owners and managers.

sentence: This is to ensure that banks that are planning to provide these services, have in place a risk management process that is adequate to assess, control and monitor associated risks.

sentence: This brings into focus our own valuable contribution to the development of sustainable microfinance as an anti-poverty tool.

label: neutral

sentence: On behalf of Governor Rafael B. label: neutral

label: positive

sentence: Credit card receivables for example, grew by an average 25.2 percent annually from 2000 to 2003.
label: positive

sentence: This has allowed more ordinary people without access to credit to now have access and on clean basis.

sentence: As a result, past due credit card accounts rose an average 40.4 percent, a pace much faster than the new ones granted.
label: negative

sentence: The number of credit card-related complaints also rose dramatically.
label: negative

sentence: It is in this regard that we should work hand in hand in channeling more efforts towards customer education and protection.

label: neutral

sentence: It is my pleasure to be invited to deliver this keynote speech in this 2 nd International Forum on Microfinance sponsored by INAFI Philippines.
label: neutral

sentence: I welcome this opportunity to share with you the progress that we have achieved thus far in promoting microfinance in the banking sector.
label: positive

sentence: In year 2000, the BSP took up the challenge of promoting the development of sustainable microfinance.
label: neutral

sentence: Since then we have focused on creating an enabling regulatory policy, supporting training and capacity building, drumming up support from potential practitioners, and strengthening working ties with other institutions for a broader microfinance network.
label: positive

sentence: Through these various initiatives, the banking sector has been able to directly contribute to the growth of sustainable and viable microfinance institutions.
label: positive

sentence: In our own little way, we have been able to make a positive transformational impact in the fight against poverty.
label: positive

sentence: Translated into numbers, the banking system has so far provided a total of P2.9 billion microfinance loans to 485,136 micro-borrowers as of end-May 2004.
label: positive

sentence: All in all, we have 2 microfinance-oriented thrift banks, 4 microfinance-oriented rural banks and 115 rural and cooperative rural banks with some level of microfinance operations that finance the credit requirements of the entrepreneurial poor (e-poor).
label: positive

sentence: We anticipate further expansion in microfinance activities in the banking sector judging by the applications for microfinance-oriented banks that are currently in the pipeline, and by the number of banks that have expressed their intention to set up branches for their microfinance operations.
label: positive

sentence: Even as we strongly encourage the establishment of more microfinance-oriented banks, our focus is not merely confined to statistics.
label: neutral

sentence: We also aim to provide the appropriate policy and regulatory environment for continued viability and sustainability of microfinance institutions.
label: positive

sentence: Without sound financial and credit policies, microfinance can never be an effective intervention for poverty alleviation.
label: neutral

sentence: With this goal in mind, we are building up on our current initiatives to mainstream microfinance within the BSP and the banking community.
label: positive

sentence: BSP microfinance agenda Creating an enabling policy and regulatory environment High on the list of BSP's agenda on microfinance is the continuous review of existing policies and regulatory guidelines to make them even more attuned to the growing needs of the microfinance sector.
label: positive

sentence: We believe that with a well-defined vision and strategy for microfinance, we can easily identify and fully address the capacity development needs of microfinance players.
label: positive

sentence: Initially, we have defined our policy agenda which is to encourage the establishment of new microfinance-oriented thrift and rural banks and the setting up of microfinance operations in existing banks.
label: positive

sentence: Through this approach, the banking system can more effectively reach a larger number of e-poor.
label: positive

sentence: This would complement other microfinance delivery channels like credit cooperatives and NGOs that have their own strengths and weaknesses.
label: positive

sentence: Three important provisions in the General Banking Law (GBL) of 2000 formally authorized the BSP to fully support microfinance.
label: neutral

sentence: In carrying out this mandate, we have drawn up a set of banking regulations that take into consideration the peculiarities of microfinance lending: cash flow-based, typically unsecured and short-term, high frequency collections, and often featuring group guarantee and other non-traditional forms of security.
label: neutral

sentence: Specifically, the BSP has issued the following circulars: Circular 272 (January 2001) implements the provisions of Sections 40, 43 and 44 of the GBL and sets out the regulatory guidelines for engaging in microfinance.
label: neutral

sentence: Circular 273 prescribes additional requirements for licensing of microfinance-oriented banks.
label: neutral

sentence: This was issued primarily to encourage banks to adhere to best practices and high performance standards.
label: positive

sentence: Another requirement is for the organizers to have a good track record in microfinancing.
label: neutral

sentence: As a minimum operational requirement, banks should have a loan tracking system in place that can adequately monitor and manage a microfinance portfolio.
label: neutral

sentence: The same circular also provided for the partial lifting of the moratorium on the opening of new thrift and rural banks and branches to allow the entry of microfinance-oriented banks.
label: positive

sentence: Circular 282 (April 2001) specifies the guidelines governing the rediscounting facility for microfinance loans of rural and cooperative banks.
label: neutral

sentence: Circular 324, issued in 2002, expanded the coverage of the rediscounting facility to include thrift banks.
label: positive

sentence: In relation to this, we have provided rediscounting for 26,966 micro-borrowers amounting to P219.5 million, as of end-July 2004.
label: positive

sentence: We have issued several other regulations to provide further incentive to banks to engage in microfinance in a sustainable and prudent manner.
label: positive

sentence: These are: Circular 340, circular 365, and Circular 369 which further liberalized the rules and regulations for the establishment of bank branches provided these are microfinance-oriented.
label: positive

sentence: Circular 409 prescribes guidelines for the measurement of portfolio-at-risk and the respective provisioning requirements to ensure sound and prudential microfinance operations of banks.
label: positive

sentence: To complement these regulatory initiatives, we are also strengthening our ability to supervise the activity of microfinance-oriented banks.
label: positive

sentence: We are currently finalizing revisions to the BSP manual of examination to incorporate microfinance operations.
label: positive

sentence: We have started to train a core group of bank examiners knowledgeable in microfinance operations.
label: positive

sentence: We are also intensifying our efforts, in partnership with the national credit council, to set up a credible and comprehensive credit information bureau.
label: positive

sentence: We have just completed the first phase of the project which we highly think will greatly benefit microfinance practitioners, in terms of promoting stronger credit discipline and reducing the risk of credit pollution.
label: positive

sentence: Enhancing training and capacity building Another priority area in the BSP microfinance agenda is our continuing training and capacity building program for bank examiners, officers and employees, as well as the microfinance practitioners, in the banking sector.
label: positive

sentence: This is being implemented through a series of trainings, workshops and study tours .
label: positive

sentence: For the benefit of the banking sector, the BSP has begun incorporating microfinance into the basic rural and thrift banking courses .So far, these courses have already been offered to 1,500 participants .
label: positive

sentence: In June 2004, we launched a series of seminars designed to educate banks on the importance of internal controls, performance standards and best practices for sound microfinance operations.
label: positive

sentence: To further augment our capacity to effectively supervise microfinance activities within the banking sector, we have created a top-level microfinance committee to oversee BSP's microfinance initiatives.
label: positive

sentence: Likewise, we have set up a microfinance unit within the BSP to implement and coordinate all microfinance activities.
label: positive

sentence: We are among the first central banks in the Asia-Pacific region with a permanent office dedicated to microfinance.
label: positive

sentence: Encouraging promotion and advocacy Our ongoing initiative on microfinance is also focused on promotion and advocacy.
label: positive

sentence: In 2003, we launched a regional advocacy program where we conducted microfinance seminars in strategic regions in the country.
label: positive

sentence: To date, we have been successful in conducting information seminars for a total of 1,350 participants in 9 regions across the country.
label: positive

sentence: Our advocacy program is aimed at encouraging potential practitioners from the banking, cooperative and NGO sectors, as well as establishing links with corporations, foundations and international organizations that could lend support to existing microfinance institutions (MFIs) by way of technical assistance and other capacity development needs.
label: positive

sentence: All of these initiatives underscore BSP's strong commitment to promote the development of microfinance.
label: positive

sentence: Concluding remarks Let me just point out that although the BSP's mandate is to focus on the banking sector, our involvement in microfinance extends beyond the banking community.
label: positive

sentence: We are also interested in working with other microfinance practitioners and organizations, such as NGOs and credit cooperatives, so that together we can ensure the long-term viability and sustainability of microfinance.
label: positive

sentence: Possible areas for collaboration include the transformation of non-bank MFIs into banks, development of a uniform set of standards, and the promotion of best practices for sustainable operations within banks, NGOs and cooperatives.
label: neutral

sentence: It is vital that we pool our resources if we are to achieve progress in this crusade against poverty.
label: neutral

sentence: On that note, I would like to wish everyone a pleasant day and a meaningful discussion ahead.
label: neutral

sentence: Thank you.

sentence: Introduction I have been asked, in my capacity as member of the Government's Economic Management Team, to speak to you today about the new tax initiatives of the Arroyo administration.
label: neutral

sentence: I can understand SEIPI's interest in the Administration's planned tax initiatives.
label: neutral

sentence: Taxes, after all, are a part of business costs.
label: neutral

sentence: And just as Keynes used to argue, there are only two great certainties in this world: death and taxes!
label: neutral

sentence: Your Government proposes to expand the tax base and increase tax collection.
label: neutral

sentence: This is the bad news.
label: negative

sentence: The good news is that doing so, while admittedly painful in the short term, would arrest the incredibly large decline in the tax effort ratio and the significant climb in public sector debt.
label: positive

sentence: Let me cite a few numbers.
label: neutral

sentence: The budget deficit of the National Government has been on a rising trend in the past decade, from less than 1 percent of GDP in 1995 to 5.2 percent of GDP in 2002.
label: negative

sentence: This was driven in recent years by higher-than-programmed expenditures and, more importantly, revenue shortfalls due to below-target tax collections, changes or lack of changes in tax policy, and to some extent, reductions in the tariff duties on imports.
label: neutral

sentence: [1] the overall result has been a rising level of Government debt, as the Government resorted to borrowing to compensate for the financing gap.
label: negative

sentence: The total stock of debt for the National Government alone has grown from P1.2 trillion or 61 percent of GDP in 1995 to P3.4 trillion or 77 percent in 2003.
label: negative

sentence: Such levels of fiscal deficit and public debt are generally considered to be high for an emerging economy like the Philippines.
label: negative

sentence: Unless we can tame the fiscal deficit and reduce our public debt, our growth prospects for the medium term are significantly at risk.
label: negative

sentence: You would not want to see recession and reduced capacity of your Government to provide the general public with basic public goods and services!
label: negative

sentence: The message is clear.
label: neutral

sentence: Reducing the fiscal deficit and public debt is the most urgent and critical task facing our country today, and raising fiscal revenues should be the Government's main strategy for achieving this.
label: positive

sentence: It will enable the government to have enough funds for key budget items such as infrastructure, education, health and other basic services.
label: positive

sentence: Infrastructure spending, in particular, is important for enhancing our international competitiveness and ability to achieve sustained growth over the medium term.
label: positive

sentence: Closing the fiscal gap will also help raise the level of national saving, free up funds for investments, prevent a buildup of inflation expectations, reduce domestic interest rates, and restore investor confidence in the country's macroeconomic fundamentals.
label: positive

sentence: Let me clarify an important issue of tax administration.
label: neutral

sentence: While we expand the tax base, we shall not stop intensifying tax administration.
label: positive

sentence: We shall address the issue of graft and corruption as much as we shall run after tax cheaters and evaders to plug the reportedly large tax leakage.
label: positive

sentence: And so, with due apologies to my colleagues at the Department of Finance, let me now describe to you, in broad terms, the package of fiscal measures being planned by the government.
label: neutral

sentence: Sustaining macroeconomic stability 2.1 medium-term objectives The new tax initiatives are best viewed as part of a broad, comprehensive package of fiscal measures aimed at the following objectives: (1) achieving a balanced NG budget in six years; (2) reducing the CPSD from more than 6 percent in 2003 to 3 percent of GDP in six years; (3) reducing the public sector debt in line with the NG deficit and CPSD reduction; and (4) meeting these objectives without sacrificing economic growth targets.
label: positive

sentence: As I have indicated earlier, such goals require a two-pronged action plan that consists of, firstly, raising revenues through improved tax administration, adjustments in tax policies as well as new revenue measures, and secondly, controlling public spending through austerity measures, civil service reform, and better governance of GOCCs.
label: neutral

sentence: 2.2 new tax policy measures Let me now run quickly through the new tax policy measures of the Government.
label: neutral

sentence: The new measures consist of, firstly, legislative reforms designed to increase fiscal revenues and secondly, administrative reforms.
label: neutral

sentence: The proposed amendments to existing tax laws are along the following lines: A two-step increase in the value-added tax (VAT) rate; Re-imposition of franchise tax on telecommunication companies; Adoption of a gross income tax system for corporations & business income-earning individuals; Rationalization of the system of fiscal incentives; Indexation of the excise tax on so-called "sin products"; Granting of a general tax amnesty conditional on the submission of statements of assets, liabilities and net worth (SALN); Establishment of a lateral attrition system; and Increase of the excise tax on petroleum products.
label: neutral

sentence: These reforms are mainly designed to stem the leakages in the tax system that have weakened fiscal revenue-generation over the years.
label: positive

sentence: Fiscal incentives, for example, will be rationalized on the basis of how much they actually contribute to new investments.
label: positive

sentence: Meanwhile, the proposed indexation of excise taxes will address the decline in the real value of excise tax revenues due to inflation.
label: positive

sentence: Such measures have long been proposed by fiscal experts and economists, and the economic team believes that they are quite overdue.
label: positive

sentence: Proposed administrative tax reforms include the following: Upward adjustments in the fees and charges of government agencies; Increase in duty on petroleum products from 3 percent to 5 percent, through an executive order; and Other measures to enhance the revenue-generating potential of selected GOCCs, e.g.
label: neutral

sentence: PEA, PAGCOR, etc.
label: neutral

sentence: These measures are expected to bring in some p10 billion in additional fiscal revenues.
label: positive

sentence: 2.3 fiscal discipline measures The other half of the fiscal deficit problem is government spending.
label: neutral

sentence: Fiscal authorities intend to bring fiscal expenditures under control through the following: (1) fiscal austerity measures, aimed at reducing non-essential expenditures; (2) civil service reform, aimed primarily at the rationalization of personnel services costs in the budget; and (3) improvement of management and control of the GOCCs and other government corporate entities (OGCEs).
label: positive

sentence: Proposed austerity measures include the abolition of inter-agency bodies not created by law; the giving of compensatory time-off for employees in lieu of overtime pay; the suspension of foreign and local travel; and the imposition of cost-cutting measures on utilities and supplies.
label: positive

sentence: Measures on civil service reforms begin with the issuance of executive orders to trim the bureaucracy.
label: neutral

sentence: Functions that are either no longer relevant or redundant will be abolished, and employees will be offered a voluntary but time-bound separation scheme.
label: positive

sentence: Authorities will also be submitting for Congress' approval a reengineering bill to reorganize the entire executive branch, including GOCCs, and offer the appropriate incentives to employees.
label: positive

sentence: Also in the pipeline are bills to rationalize the government compensation and retirement system.
label: positive

sentence: The final item on the expenditure agenda is improvement of management and control of GOCCs, whose financial operations bear significantly on the public sector bottom line.
label: positive

sentence: An executive order is expected to be issued that will (1) improve oversight of GOCCs by their supervising departments or agencies as well as by the Office of the President; (2) establish a binding set of corporate duties and responsibilities for all presidential appointees to boards of GOCCs/ogces; and (3) impose a moratorium on the creation of subsidiary GOCCs pending an assessment of the operations of existing subsidiary GOCCs.
label: positive

sentence: Concluding remarks In closing, I believe that our ability to reverse our poor fiscal position will be a key factor in determining the pace and sustainability of economic growth over the next several years.
label: positive

sentence: Our ability to balance the budget and reduce our public borrowing will determine how much funds will be available for public investments, and how much resources will be allocated to key economic needs such as infrastructure, education and training, health services, and so on.
label: positive

sentence: A better fiscal performance will also be a key factor in strengthening investor confidence in the Philippine economy.
label: positive

sentence: Concerns about the government's ability to manage the deficit partly prevents foreign capital flows, since investors think that the government may not have enough funds for the upgrading and maintenance of public infrastructure.
label: negative

sentence: Good public infrastructure, after all, is a key ingredient to a sound business environment.
label: neutral

sentence: Reducing the public deficit to me is therefore one of the urgent and critical tasks for the government.
label: neutral

sentence: Raising revenues through tax initiatives is a crucial part of the strategy to bring the deficit under control.
label: positive

sentence: Let us bear in mind, therefore, that the Government's tax initiatives are part of the Government's macroeconomic program to secure our economic growth prospects for the medium term.
label: positive

sentence: The other pillars of this program is the reform of the power sector, the reform of the financial sector, including the development of the capital market, and buildup in infrastructure and external competitiveness.
label: positive

sentence: It is, to be sure, a very difficult program, one that will require the support of every Filipino.
label: neutral

sentence: And because the fiscal budget affects not only the delivery of government services but also inflation and national income, every Filipino has a stake in ensuring that our fiscal objectives for the medium term are achieved.
label: positive

sentence: Always remember, no pain, no gain!
label: positive

sentence: We can help in these efforts not only through supporting the government's tax initiatives but also pushing our authorities and legislators to exercise fiscal responsibility and discipline.
label: positive

sentence: The next journey So what is Basel 2?
label: neutral

sentence: Basel 2 is a set of proposals that aim to revise Basel 1 to make regulatory capital more risk sensitive and reflective of all, or at least most of the risks banks are exposed to.
label: neutral

sentence: As such, Basel 2 is structured as a three-pronged approach that transcends regulatory capital requirements.
label: positive

sentence: That is, Basel 2 not only presents us a risk-based capital framework, but an entire risk-based supervisory framework.
label: positive

sentence: The three pillars of Basel 2 are (1) minimum capital requirements (pillar 1), (2) supervisory review process (pillar 2), and (3) market discipline (pillar 3).
label: neutral

sentence: Pillar 1 sets out the detailed guidelines for computing regulatory capital for credit risk, market risk, and operational risk.
label: positive

sentence: Major changes are introduced with respect to credit risk.
label: neutral

sentence: Basel 2 presents three options: the Standardized Approach, the Foundation Internal Ratings Based Approach, and the advanced Internal Ratings Based (IRB) approach.
label: neutral

sentence: The standardized approach assigns risk weights applicable to sovereign, bank, corporate, and retail exposures.
label: neutral

sentence: Depending on the external credit risk ratings.
label: neutral

sentence: The foundation IRB approach, on the other hand, allows banks to use their internal ratings assessment to determine the appropriate capital charge of their exposures.
label: positive

sentence: The advanced IRB approach is very similar to the foundation IRB.
label: positive

sentence: The only difference is that under the advanced IRB, banks are allowed even greater discretion in using their internal ratings model to calculate capital requirements.
label: positive

sentence: Capital treatment for market risk as currently implemented through Circular No.
label: neutral

sentence: 360, remains generally unchanged except for some minor changes in the standardized computation of specific risk charges.
label: neutral

sentence: Whereas, specific risk weights in the current market risk framework depend on the type of issuer, Basel 2 requires that these now depend on the external ratings of the issue to be consistent with the credit risk standardized approach.
label: positive

sentence: An explicit change for operational risk is a new element.
label: positive

sentence: Basel 2 presents three options: the basic indicator approach, the standardized approach, and the advanced measurement approaches.
label: neutral

sentence: Under the basic indicator approach, a bank's operational risk charge is computed as a fraction of its gross income.
label: neutral

sentence: Under the standardized approach, banks operations are divided into specific business lines.
label: neutral

sentence: Operational risk charge for each business line is determined as a fraction of gross income attributed to each line.
label: neutral

sentence: Lastly, operational risk charge under the advanced measurement approaches would depend on statistics-based advanced measurement models developed by banks themselves or by outside vendors.
label: neutral

sentence: Pillar 2 of Basel 2, on the other hand, prescribes sound principles that should be followed in the process of supervisory review.
label: positive

sentence: It works on the principle that banks can take on risk, but the board of directors and senior management should be responsible to assess the appropriate capital requirement in relation to its risk exposures, including exposures to risks not captured in pillar 1.
label: positive

sentence: Banks therefore will be asked to present to the supervisory body their internal capital allocation process.
label: positive

sentence: Again, this is where strong corporate governance comes into play supervisors are then expected to be able to evaluate the process by which banks came up with their "appropriate" capital measures in order to intervene at an early stage before capital goes below what is prudently acceptable.
label: positive

sentence: Pillar 3 of Basel 2, meanwhile, proposes an extensive list of bank disclosure requirements.
label: positive

sentence: It recognizes that markets contain disciplinary mechanisms that can reinforce the efforts of supervisors by rewarding banks that manage risk effectively and penalizing those whose risk management is inept or imprudent.
label: positive

sentence: Pillar 3, therefore, is envisaged to result in well-founded counter-party risk assessments.
label: positive

sentence: In addition, pillar 3 is also envisaged to support efforts to foster strong corporate governance within banks.
label: positive

sentence: In summary, Basel 2 is based on the principles that (1) banks should have capital appropriate for their risk-taking activities, (2) banks should be able to properly assess the risk it is taking and supervisors should be able to evaluate the soundness of these assessments, and (3) banks should be disclosing pertinent information necessary to enable market mechanism to complement the supervisory oversight function.
